conversations.say: This strip shows a recurring private habit, continuity, several collectors, and a disputed change—not proof of one customer.
conversations.offer: I’ll show you O06 and explain the transaction context.
conversations.request: Use it only as an attributed pattern; do not identify customers or claim it proves who R was.

conversations.say: My ledger contains side arrangements that are nobody else’s entertainment.
conversations.offer: I will not disclose your local transactions or speculate about your identity.
conversations.request: Promise the same boundary for my customers and the altered entries: discuss patterns, not names or arrangements.

conversations.say: I’m choosing to forgive the old R-associated balance publicly instead of selling a single-customer legend.
conversations.offer: I’ll close the account without payment and keep neighborhood names out of the story.
conversations.request: Publicly support that choice and our rule that transaction patterns do not identify R.

public_statement: The outstanding R-associated balance is forgiven. The ledger records continuity, but money moving through an account does not establish one sender or one customer. Nobody gets to buy the neighborhood’s story from me.

commitments.promise: I will provide bounded transaction context if you preserve attribution and customer anonymity.
We will disclose transaction patterns but not customer identities or side arrangements.
I will forgive the outstanding balance and protect neighborhood names if you publicly support that choice.

goal_progress: Publicly forgave the outstanding R-associated balance.
Sought C16’s support for forgiveness and neighborhood control.
Offered Morgan a bounded ledger pattern for concrete attribution limits.
Proposed a mutual disclosure boundary with Dee.
reasoning_summary: The recurring habit suggests continuity, while several collectors and a disputed change weaken any clean single-person story. The article remains unestablished, and Vale’s absence supplies no reliable cause or identity evidence.
